name: Sinus Node Dysfunction
identifyBy: Symptomatic sinus bradycardia - rate <40 bpm with symptoms (syncope, presyncope)
Sinus pauses >3 seconds - prolonged periods without any electrical activity
Chronotropic incompetence: failure to increase heart rate with exertion
Tachy-brady pattern: alternating fast (AF/flutter) and slow (sinus pause after conversion)
Sinoatrial exit block: regular P wave dropped creating a pause equal to 2x (or 3x) normal P-P interval

measurements.pWaves: Present when sinus, absent during pauses

management: Assess haemodynamic stability and symptom severity
Transcutaneous pacing if atropine fails or pauses >5 seconds with symptoms
Stop any causative drugs: beta-blockers, calcium channel blockers, digoxin, amiodarone
Urgent evacuation if symptomatic pauses or syncopal episodes
pearls: Sick sinus syndrome most commonly presents as unexplained syncope in older adults
Tachy-brady syndrome: AF terminates → long sinus pause → syncope (the pause is the dangerous event)
Chronotropic incompetence is underdiagnosed - suspect if HR fails to reach 85% predicted max during stress
External pacing pads should be applied even before symptoms worsen
Reversible causes: medications, hypothyroidism, hypothermia, inferior MI - always check first

qaNotes: Sinus rhythm family: P before every QRS unless sinus-node dysfunction pause pattern is specified.
